HRREC-91981.summary: PH: HRREC-91946 [Initialisation] Recruiter can see Email Task on SSP (XO Only)
HRREC-91981.description:
h2. User story

*As a* recruiter
*I want* to see the Email task on SSP as delivered by the XO initialisation track
*So that* I can enter the email =xperience from the standard task surface

h2. Scenarios

h3. Scenario 1: Task visible when initialisation complete

*Given* HRREC-91946 deliverables are deployed for my tenant
*When* I open SSP for an in-scope candidate context
*Then* I can see the Email task per XO configuration

h3. Scenario 2: Task hidden when not in scope

*Given* my tenant is not configured for this task surface
*When* I open SSP
*Then* I do not see a broken or partial Email task entry

h3. Scenario 3: Task present but channel disabled

*Given* the Email task exists
*And* admin disabled 2-way email channel
*When* I open the task
*Then* behaviour matches combined XO + channel policy (no dead compose loop)

HRREC-91985.summary: PH: Recruiter can compose an email to non-agency candidate
HRREC-91985.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter communicating with a non-agency candidate
*I want to* compose 2-way emails only when the candidate is opted in and I have "Modify/Send" access to the Conversational Email security domain,
*So that* I maintain data compliance and am not shown compose controls I cannot use.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Compose for authorised, opted-in non-agency candidates

*Given* the non-agency candidate is opted in to receive emails and I have "Modify" access to the Conversational Email security domain
*And* I have opened the 2-way email task in the sliding side panel
*When* I can click the Add (Plus) or Reply buttons (if available) to open the compose view.
*Then* I see the compose view, where I can enter the body, subject, and send to the permitted recipient
h3. Scenario 2: Compose controls are hidden when a non-agency candidate is not opted in

*Given* the non-agency candidate has not opted in (or has opted out) to receive emails 
*When* I open the 2-way email task in the sliding side panel 
*Then* the compose controls (such as the Add/Compose buttons) are completely hidden from the recruiter, as I am not permitted to send a new email.
h3. Scenario 3: Compose controls are hidden for read-only security access

*Given* my security role grants me "View" access but lacks "Modify" access to the Conversational Email domain
*When* I open the 2-way email task in the sliding side panel for a non-agency candidate
*Then* the compose controls (Add and Reply) are hidden based on conditional logic on the UI, allowing me to view historical messages without the ability to author new ones.

HRREC-91992.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want to* be stopped clearly and immediately by the UI when I try to attach non-supported or oversized files,
*So that* I do not accidentally send insecure attachments or exceed system limits, saving me from backend delivery failures.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Client-side block for disallowed file extensions

*Given* I attempt to select an attachment from my local system
*When* the file is a disallowed or executable type (e.g., .exe, .bat, .js) as defined by the tenant setup
*Then* the UI immediately rejects the file upon selection
*And* I see a clear client-side error message explaining that the file type is not supported
*And* the file is not staged in the email draft.
h3. Scenario 2: Single file exceeds maximum configured size

*Given* I attempt to select an attachment from my local system
*When* the individual file size exceeds the maximum configured attachment size limit
*Then* the UI immediately rejects the file
*And* I see size-specific guidance in the error message (e.g., "File exceeds the maximum limit of X MB").
h3. Scenario 3: Cumulative attachments exceed total email size limit

*Given* I have already successfully staged one or more valid attachments to my email draft
*When* I attempt to add another file that would push the total combined email size (body + all attachments) over the system's maximum payload limit (e.g., 30 MB)
*Then* the UI prevents the newest file from being attached
*And* I see an error message indicating that the total email size limit has been reached.

HRREC-91993.summary: PH: Recruiter sees UI error validations on Send
HRREC-91993.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want* the system to perform inline validation when I click "Send" on an email draft,
*So that* I am prevented from dispatching messages with missing required fields (like To, Subject) and avoid failed backend round-trips.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Blocked send due to missing Subject

*Given* the "Subject" field is empty
*When* I click the "Send" button
*Then* I see inline/field-level validation "Error: Required field." indicating the subject is required, and the email dispatch is blocked.
h3. Scenario 2: Blocked send due to empty "To" field

*Given* the "To" dropdown field is empty (no recipient is selected)
*When* I click the "Send" button
*Then* I see inline/field-level validation "Error: Required field." indicating a recipient is required, and the email dispatch is blocked.
h3. Scenario 3: Multiple validation errors trigger a blocking alert

*Given* multiple required fields are missing or in an invalid state (e.g., the "To" dropdown is empty AND the "Subject" field is empty)
*When* I click the "Send" button
*Then* the email dispatch is blocked
*And* I see a standard red blocking alert summarising the total number of issues (e.g., "2 Errors"), which guides me to correct all invalid fields before the system will allow a successful round-trip.

HRREC-91994.description:
h2. User story

*As a* Recruiter
*I want to* send my composed email with no attachments and see the outbound message appear in-thread with its updated delivery state
*So that* I have a trusted, auditable record of my communication history 
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Send and in-thread display (No Attachments)

*Given* a valid composed email state containing no attachments
*When* I click the "Send" button
*Then* the message text payload is successfully dispatched to the backend via REST API
*And* the message immediately appears in the sliding side panel conversation thread with a "Sent" status tag.
h3. Scenario 2: Client polling for delayed visibility

*Given* I have successfully dispatched a message via the REST API
*When* there is a delay in the backend processing making the message visible in the thread return data
*Then* the client UI handles this by polling/retrying the GET methods so the message correctly appears in the thread without requiring a manual page refresh.
h3. Scenario 3: Duplicate send prevention

*Given* I am clicking the "Send" button
*When* the REST API call is in flight
*Then* the client automatically disables the "Send" button to prevent accidental double-taps from creating duplicate logical messages in the backend

HRREC-92001.summary: PH: Recruiter sees the panel expand & views full message when clicking the grow button or clicking a single message
HRREC-92001.description:
h2. Story 1: M5 - Expand Panel to Wide View (Structural / UI Framework)

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want* the sliding side panel to dynamically expand to a wide reading layout when I click "Grow" or open a single message,
*So that* long-form emails are comfortably readable compared to the narrow view used for SMS or Notes.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Panel expansion on standard screens (Modal & Drop Shadow)

*Given* the sliding side panel is open in the narrow view on a standard screen
*When* an expand event is dispatched (via clicking the "Grow" button or a single message)
*Then* the panel expands to the wide layout
*And* a modal/drop shadow appears on the rest of the page behind the panel to focus user attention.
h3. Scenario 2: "Overlay" behavior on Extra Large (XL) screens

*Given* the panel is open and the main page content is currently "pushed" beside it due to an XL screen resolution
*When* an expand event is dispatched to grow the panel
*Then* the panel expands and safely overlays the main page content rather than attempting to push the content further off-screen.
h3. Scenario 3: Toggling back to the narrow view (Shrink)

*Given* the sliding side panel is currently expanded to the wide view
*When* the user triggers a shrink event by clicking the action again or switching to another channel (like SMS)
*Then* the panel toggles back to its standard narrow width and the background drop shadow is removed.

HRREC-92003.summary: PH: Recruiter sees error messages if their email has bounced
HRREC-92003.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want* clear bounce diagnostics on my affected outbound messages in the sliding side panel,
*So that* I can quickly understand why my email was not delivered and correct any addressable issues
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Generic / Default Not-Delivered (Hard Bounce)

*Given* an outbound email fails to deliver and the provider returns a standard hard bounce event
*When* I view the message in the expanded wide view
*Then* I see the headline: "We couldn't deliver this email."
*And* I see the body: "Try sending it again or contact the recipient using another method."
h3. Scenario 2: Spam / Sender-Reputation Variant (Complaint)

*Given* the failure is classified as a complaint (recipient marked a previous email as spam)
*When* I view the message in the expanded wide view
*Then* I see the headline: "We couldn't deliver this email."
*And* I see the body: "The recipient marked a previous email as spam. Avoid emailing this address to protect your sender reputation, and contact the recipient using another method."
h3. Scenario 3: Unsubscribe / Consent Variant (Subscription)

*Given* the failure is classified as an unsubscribe/opt-out event
*When* I view the message in the expanded wide view
*Then* I see the headline: "We couldn't deliver this email."
*And* I see the body: "The recipient unsubscribed from your emails. You'll need their explicit opt-in before you can email them again. Try contacting them using another method."
h3. Scenario 4: Virus / Unsafe Content Variant (AWS SES: Reject)

*Given* Amazon SES accepted the email but blocked delivery because the content or attachment was flagged as a virus
*When* I view the message in the expanded wide view
*Then* I see the headline: "We couldn't deliver this email."
*And* I see the body: "This email was blocked because it contained content that was flagged as a virus. Remove any attachments and try sending it again."
h3. Scenario 5: Temporary Server Delay Variant (AWS SES: DeliveryDelay)

*Given* the failure is classified as a temporary delay (e.g., recipient's inbox is full or server is unavailable)
*When* I view the message in the expanded wide view
*Then* I see the headline: "We couldn't deliver this email."
*And* I see the body: "The recipient's server is temporarily unavailable. We'll automatically keep trying, but if it's urgent, contact them using another method."
h3. Scenario 6: Technical Issue Variant (Catch-All)

*Given* the failure is classified as a technical issue which did not compirse of any of the variants in scenarios 1-5
*When* I view the message in the expanded wide view
*Then* I see the headline: "We couldn't deliver this email."
*And* I see the body: "We couldn't deliver your email because of a technical issue. If the issue persists, use another method to reach the recipient or contact support."

HRREC-92004.summary: PH: Recruiter sees error when sending message (REST error)
HRREC-92004.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want* structured UI error handling when my email fails to send due to a REST API error,
*So that* I understand why the send was blocked and what manual actions I need to take to resolve the issue.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Mid-session state change blocks send (Opt-out or Security Loss)

*Given* I am actively drafting a message in the sliding side panel
*When* I click "Send" but the REST endpoint fails because the underlying system state changed (e.g., the candidate opted out or security access was revoked)
*Then* the send is completely blocked and automated retries are not attempted
*And* I see a clear error message explaining the change in status and instructing me to copy my email message, and then to refresh the page.
h3. Scenario 2: Generic system/network failure during send

*Given* I attempt to send a drafted email
*When* the REST API fails due to a generic server error, timeout, or network issue (e.g., 500-level error)
*Then* the UI safely blocks the send and displays a standard error message indicating the system could not process the request.

HRREC-92005.summary: PH: Recruiter sees info message and cannot send email if the candidate changes their email
HRREC-92005.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want to* be blocked from sending new messages and see a clear explanation if a candidate's primary home email address changes mid-thread,
*So that* I do not accidentally send communications to an outdated or incorrect address, ensuring data privacy and accuracy.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Candidate email change hides actions and displays an info message

*Given* an active 2-way email conversation exists with a candidate
*When* the candidate's primary home email address changes in the system (e.g., via a profile merge)
*Then* the "Reply" and "Add/New" buttons are completely hidden from the UI
*And* I see a clear informational message in the sliding side panel explaining that the candidate's email has changed and new messages cannot be sent in this thread.
h3. Scenario 2: Historical messages remain accessible as read-only

*Given* a conversation is blocked due to an email address change
*When* I view the thread history in the sliding side panel
*Then* I can still read the full history of prior messages sent under the old email address to preserve the audit trail
*But* the system prevents me from replying or adding new messages to this specific conversation thread.

*So that* Recruiters cannot send new mail but conversation is not fully closed (transcript with DUB)

*When* I view the conversation

h3. Scenario 2: Not fully closed state

*Given* opt-out in-between state applies
*Then* conversation is not marked fully closed per transcript semantics

h3. Scenario 3: Re-opt-in restores send

*Given* candidate re-opts in per policy
*When* I refresh conversation state
*Then* reply/add return if business rules allow

HRREC-92007.description:

*I want to* use tabs in the sliding side panel to switch between candidate and agency email threads (for agency-submitted job applications),
*So that* I can seamlessly manage formal agency coordination while keeping candidate and agency communications strictly isolated.

*And* switching to the "Candidate" tab shows strictly Recruiter ↔ Candidate emails
*And* switching to the "Agency" tab shows strictly Recruiter ↔ Agency emails

HRREC-92008.summary: PH: Agency cannot see recruiter conversation
HRREC-92008.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiting Agency User,
*I want* my conversation view to be strictly scoped to only the email threads involving myself and the recruiter,
*So that* I cannot view recruiter-to-candidate communications or internal evaluation artifacts, maintaining enterprise confidentiality practices.

*Context:*

Agency users must only see the emails sent between the recruiter and the agency. They must never see messages sent directly between the recruiter and the candidate.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Agency user thread list is strictly scoped

*Given* I am logged in as a recruiting agency user
*When* I view the communication history for a candidate I have submitted
*Then* I only see the specific email threads sent between myself (or my agency) and the internal recruiter.
h3. Scenario 2: Unauthorized direct thread access is prevented (Security)

*Given* I am authenticated as an agency user
*When* I attempt to directly access the thread ID for a Recruiter-to-Candidate conversation (e.g., via direct object reference)
*Then* I am denied access and receive a standard error (e.g., 403 Forbidden or 404 Not Found) without any conversational content leaking.

HRREC-92009.summary: PH: Recruiter can see both closed and non-closed conversation in the same panel if one conversation is still active
HRREC-92009.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want* candidate and agency conversations to be isolated in separate tabs with independent active/closed states,
*So that* the system can handle open and closed states simultaneously, as conversations with the candidate may close at a different time than those with the agency.

*Context:* 

Independent Lifecycles: A a candidate's conversation might formally close based on disposition or hire rules, while the agency conversation remains open for further administrative coordination.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Independent closure lifecycles update tabs dynamically

*Given* the candidate and agency conversations are on different lifecycles, and the candidate conversation formally closes before the agency one, or vice-versa
*When* I click between the two tabs in the sliding side panel
*Then* the UI dynamically updates the available actions:
 * The *closed conversation tab* hides the "Reply" and "Add/Plus" buttons, leaving a read-only historical view and an informational message.

 * The *active conversation tab* displays all compose buttons normally.

HRREC-92010.description:

*I want to* see clear status indicators (such as Sent, Not Delivered) for my 2-way email messages in the sliding side panel,
*So that* I can trust the delivery state of my communications

*When* the recruiter views the conversation thread in the sliding side panel
*Then* the message displays a "Sent" tag to indicate it has been passed to the backend email provider.
h3. Scenario 2: Post-send outbound delivery error shows a "Not Delivered" tag

*Given* an outbound email fails to reach the recipient after being sent (e.g., it bounces back or is blocked for containing a virus)

*Then* a "Not Delivered" error message is visibly surfaced on the specific message in the UI.

*When* scheduler runs
*Then* conversation is closed automatically with audit

*Then* validation prevents save with clear message

HRREC-92014.summary: PH: Recruiter cannot see reply, or Add button when both conversations are closed & sees info message? that convo is closed
HRREC-92014.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want* clear UI indicators when a conversation has been formally closed due to system lifecycle rules,
*So that* I understand why I can no longer send messages and can rely on the thread as a read-only historical record.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Formally closed conversation hides actions and shows an info message

*Given* a 2-way email conversation has reached its terminal closed state (e.g., 1-year hard stop or retention period reached)
*When* I view the conversation in the sliding side panel
*Then* the "Reply" and "Add/New" buttons are completely hidden from the UI
*And* I see a clear informational message indicating that the conversation is permanently closed.
h3. Scenario 2: Independent closure of Candidate vs. Agency threads

*Given* I am managing both a candidate and an agency user email conversation for the same job application
*When* one conversation formally closes (e.g., the agency thread) but the other remains active
*Then* only the closed conversation hides its action buttons and displays the closed info message
*And* the active conversation tab remains fully interactable.
h3. Scenario 3: Read-only access to historical threads is preserved

*Given* a conversation is formally closed
*When* I access the thread in the sliding side panel
*Then* I can still read the full chronological history of the messages for audit purposes
*But* I cannot initiate any new outbound emails.

HRREC-92015.summary: PH: Candidate has 2 applications to the same JR and gets merged
HRREC-92015.description:
h2. User story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want* my 2-way email communication threads to remain intact and safely handled when duplicate candidates are merged using the Duplicate Management Framework,
*So that* I do not lose context of previous conversations and avoid sending emails to outdated addresses post-merge.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: Post-merge displays all original conversation threads

*Given* two candidate records with active applications for the same Job Requisition are merged
*When* the recruiter views the 2-way email sliding side panel for the merged candidate
*Then* both original email conversations are moved over and visible as separate contexts, allowing the recruiter to view both and decide which thread to continue using.
h3. Scenario 2: Candidate email change during merge pauses the conversation

*Given* a candidate merge operation results in a change to the candidate's primary email address
*When* the recruiter views the active conversation threads
*Then* the system detects the email change and handles it (e.g., by pausing the conversation or surfacing an info message) to prevent the recruiter from sending new messages to the outdated address.

HRREC-92016.description:
h2. User Story

*As a* Recruiter,
*I want* inbound emails containing unsupported, oversized, or malicious attachments to be safely flagged in the conversation UI,
*So that* I am aware the candidate attempted to share a file, even if malware or system limits prevented the attachment from being delivered.
h2. Scenarios
h3. Scenario 1: UI displays a system message for blocked/unsupported file types

*Given* a candidate replies with an attachment using a system-blocked file format (e.g., .exe, .bat, .js)
*When* the UI fetches the ingested message payload from the backend
*Then* the UI displays the email body but the attachment is safely stripped
*And* an automated system message or error indicator is surfaced to the recruiter explaining the attachment was rejected.
h3. Scenario 2: UI displays a system message for virus scanner failures

*Given* a candidate sends an email containing an attachment with malware
*When* the backend virus scanning service flags and blocks the file during ingestion
*Then* the UI displays an automated system message in the conversation thread indicating that the candidate's attachment was blocked due to a virus.
h3. Scenario 3: UI surfaces a system message for oversized payload bounces

*Given* a candidate attempts to send an email where the total size exceeds the 30 MB maximum limit
*When* the backend rejects the payload and the email bounces
*Then* the UI displays an automated system message in the thread indicating that the candidate attempted to send a message, but it failed/bounced due to exceeding size limits.
